government_hackathons.full_name: D.E.S.I.G.N. for BioE3 Challenge: Empowering Youth to Solve Critical Issues of their TIMES
government_hackathons.theme: Biotechnology innovation for sustainability and self-reliance
government_hackathons.eligibility_criteria: Category 1: School students (Classes VI-XII) enrolled in any Indian school. Category 2: All Indian citizens including students, researchers, startups, faculty, and individuals. Category 2 Round 2 requires affiliation with an autonomous institution, bio-incubator, or DSIR-certified startup.
government_hackathons.prizes: ₹1 lakh per winning team per monthly cycle. Winning solutions may submit full proposal for RDI grant up to ₹25 lakhs to convert design idea to Proof of Concept. Digital Certificate of Merit signed by Minister of State for Science and Technology.
government_hackathons.funding_support: Up to ₹25 lakhs RDI grant for winners
government_hackathons.incubation_support: Bio-incubator access through BRIC and BIRAC network for Category 2 winners
government_hackathons.mode: Video submission with idea proposal (≤120 seconds) or 1500-word concept proposal

government_hackathons.hackathon_name: Tribal Business Conclave 2026 — Open Grand Challenge
government_hackathons.full_name: Tribal Business Conclave 2026 — Open Grand Challenge by Ministry of Tribal Affairs
government_hackathons.theme: Transforming healthcare, education, and livelihoods in tribal communities
sdg_alignment: SDG 8 - Decent Work and Economic Growth
government_hackathons.eligibility_criteria: All DPIIT-recognised Startups across ideation, validation, early traction, and scaling stages. Individuals (solo founders) at ideation stage also eligible. No requirement to be from Scheduled Tribe category. Open to all DPIIT-recognized startups.
government_hackathons.prizes: Total prize pool ₹12 lakhs. Three winning startups receive monetary incentives.
government_hackathons.funding_support: Prize money and potential pilot support
government_hackathons.incubation_support: Structured hand-holding support and national platform for showcasing solutions
government_hackathons.mode: Online submission through Startup India AMS portal

borderline_opportunities.hackathon_name: DBT-BIRAC Bio-AI Joint Rolling Call for Proposals 2026
borderline_opportunities.full_name: DBT-BIRAC Joint Call for Proposals on Bio-AI for Establishing मूलांकुर Hubs under BioE3 Policy for Biomanufacturing
borderline_opportunities.theme: Integrating AI with biotechnology for high-performance biomanufacturing
borderline_opportunities.eligibility_criteria: Indian academia, R&D institutions, universities, companies/startups. Multi-disciplinary teams with expertise in biotechnology and/or AI/ML.
borderline_opportunities.borderline_reason: Research proposal grant call rather than traditional hackathon/competition. Structured as rolling call for establishing Bio-AI research hubs with grant funding up to ₹25 crore. Not a competitive event with winner announcement structure.

borderline_opportunities.full_name: India-Finland Joint Innovation Call 2026 by DST and Business Finland
borderline_opportunities.hosting_organization: Department of Science and Technology (DST), Technology Development Board (TDB)
borderline_opportunities.domain: Sustainable Energy, Advanced Digital Technologies, Microelectronics
borderline_opportunities.theme: Bilateral R&D collaboration for innovation and commercialization
borderline_opportunities.eligibility_criteria: Indian for-profit company registered under Companies Act with 51%+ Indian ownership. Must collaborate with a Finnish company. LLPs, sole proprietorships, OPCs not eligible.
borderline_opportunities.borderline_reason: Bilateral R&D grant funding call, not a hackathon or competitive event. Industry-driven project funding with reimbursement basis. Not open to individual participants or students.

metadata.notes: Extensive search conducted across 45+ government domains. Two genuinely open opportunities verified with official sources and active registration paths. Several other government challenges found but confirmed closed (deadlines passed). SIH 2026 and other major annual events not yet announced for next cycle. Borderline opportunities include research grant calls that do not qualify as hackathons/competitions under strict semantic classification.
